name: indigenous_land_data_sharing_conservation_grant
premise: Tribal natural resources director Aiyana Begay must decide whether to share high-resolution wildlife telemetry with a federal agency offering a conservation grant that requires open data portals. Federal liaison Dr. Paul Grant says withholding data voids funding for invasive species removal that threatens tribal fisheries. Elders fear poachers will misuse open GPS for ceremonial plant harvest sites near shared coordinates. Grant deadline is midnight; partial redaction may violate grant terms.

agents.name: Aiyana Begay
agents.identity: I am the tribal natural resources director accountable to council for data sovereignty, fisheries health, and interagency agreements.
agents.goal: I want invasive species controlled without publishing granular locations that could expose culturally sensitive lands to exploitation.

agents.name: Dr. Paul Grant
agents.identity: I am a federal biologist administering grants who must justify reproducible datasets to auditors and congressional appropriators.
agents.goal: I want complete telemetry shared so joint models can target removals before spawning runs, even if that pressures tribes on redaction.

choices: I will sign the grant with full open telemetry as written, accepting elder backlash and potential poaching exposure to secure immediate funding.
I will decline the grant unless the agency accepts aggregated telemetry with delayed release until fencing and patrol budgets pass, accepting invasive spread risk during delay.
forced_choice.call_to_action: Council is split three-three and needs Aiyana Begay's tie-breaking recommendation before midnight. Aiyana Begay must choose between full open data for the grant or declining without redaction flexibility. What does Aiyana Begay do?
